name: Minted Pears with Honey Walnuts
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons Low or no fat margarine
recipeInstructions:
1. To make honey walnuts: Butter a small cookie sheet.
2. Melt butter and honey in a medium skillet over medium heat. Add walnuts. Cook, stirring often for 5 minutes until walnuts start to carmelize. Spread nut mixture on cookie sheets to cool.
3. To make minted pears: Bring water to boiling in a large saucepan. Remove from heat. Add tea bags and 1/2 cup honey, stirring to dissolve honey. Cover and steep for 5 minutes.
4. Peel pears, leaving stems intact. Rub each with lemon juice to prevent discoloring.
5. Remove tea bags from tea. Bring tea to a simmer. Add pears. Cover and simmer for 10-20 minutes until tender. Time depends on the ripeness and size of the pears.
6. To serve: Pears may be served hot or cold in poaching syrup. Spoon 3 pears into each of 4 serving dishes (or one apiece if using larger pears). Sprinkle with honey walnuts.
